Title: ZmCCD8 regulates sugar and amino acid accumulation in maize kernels via strigolactone signalling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: kernel nitrogen accumulation and grain filling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lysine/Histidine Transporter (LHT) family amino acid transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated in ZmCCD8-OE and ZmD14B-OE kernels; down-regulated in zmccd8 kernels at 7 DAP
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: grain filling stage; kernels sampled at 7 and 21 DAP under field conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in maize; metabolite profiling of free amino acids; transcript analysis; promoter binding/repression by Y1H, EMSA, ChIP-qPCR and dual-luciferase assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes free amino acid accumulation in maize kernels without changing soluble sugar concentration
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLHT14 transcript was positively associated with ZmCCD8/strigolactone signalling in kernels. 2. ZmLHT14 is directly repressed by ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 through promoter binding. 3. ZmLHT14 overexpression increased total free amino acids in 21-DAP kernels, including alanine, β-alanine, isoleucine, lysine, methionine and threonine.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase kernel amino acid accumulation and nitrogen-use efficiency; could be combined with SL-pathway activation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper functionally validates amino acid accumulation but does not directly assay substrate specificity in planta beyond metabolite outcomes.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control gene for SL production and downstream kernel assimilate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a carotenoid-cleavage step required for strigolactone biosynthesis, promoting production of maize SLs such as zealactone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies hormonal signal that activates transport-mediated accumulation of sucrose and amino acids in kernels
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and nitrogen accumulation for grain filling, ear growth and kernel development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root and ear during flowering; transcript gradually declines in ear from silking to 3 weeks after pollination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in OE kernels; absent/reduced in CRISPR knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: phosphate starvation enhanced SL detection in roots; kernel analyses at 7 and 21 DAP under field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA PRJNA978351; GWAS using public MaizeGo dataset
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; SL quantification by UPLC-MS/MS; kernel sugar and amino acid profiling; RNA-seq; GWAS association
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone production, increases sucrose and free amino acid accumulation in kernels, and enhances ear growth traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCCD8 knockout abolished detectable root zealactone and reduced ear size, kernel weight, sucrose and amino acid accumulation. 2. ZmCCD8 overexpression increased zealactone, ear diameter/length/weight, kernel weight per ear, sucrose and amino acid accumulation. 3. ZmCCD8 acts upstream of a UB3-MYB42/MYB63-SWEET/LHT14 module controlling kernel carbon and nitrogen import. 4. Promoter SNPs in ZmCCD8 are associated with ear and cob diameter in natural maize populations.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding high-yield and nitrogen-efficient maize; favorable promoter haplotypes or expression-enhancing edits could improve ear/cob diameter and assimilate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: 100-kernel weight was unchanged in overexpression lines despite increased kernel weight per ear; broader architectural SL-related effects on shoot growth were observed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts indirectly on nutrient metabolites via SL signalling rather than directly synthesizing sugars or amino acids.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c and ZmLHT14
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone signalling via ZmCCD8 and ZmD14B; repression by ZmUB3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and nitrogen accumulation for grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in zmccd8 kernels and down-regulated in ZmCCD8-OE and ZmD14B-OE kernels at 7 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Knockout increases transporter expression in kernels at 7 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: kernel grain filling stage at 7 and 21 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; promoter reporters used in DLR assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transport-mediated kernel sucrose and amino acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator linking SL signalling to assimilate transporter repression in kernels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses kernel sucrose and amino acid accumulation; knockout increases soluble sugars and free amino acids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB42 is induced in zmccd8 and repressed in ZmCCD8-OE kernels. 2. It directly binds MBS motifs in promoters of ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c and ZmLHT14 and represses their expression. 3. Knockout of ZmMYB42 increases sucrose and free amino acid accumulation in kernels.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function editing could enhance kernel carbon and nitrogen accumulation; promising repressor target for breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of UB3 in the SL signalling cascade.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c and ZmLHT14
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone signalling via ZmCCD8 and ZmD14B; repression by ZmUB3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and nitrogen accumulation for grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in zmccd8 kernels and down-regulated in ZmCCD8-OE and ZmD14B-OE kernels at 7 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Knockout increases transporter expression in kernels at 7 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: kernel grain filling stage at 7 and 21 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; promoter reporters used in DLR assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transport-mediated kernel sucrose and amino acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator linking SL signalling to assimilate transporter repression in kernels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses kernel sucrose and amino acid accumulation; knockout increases soluble sugars and free amino acids
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB63 is induced in zmccd8 and repressed in ZmCCD8-OE kernels. 2. It directly binds MBS motifs in promoters of ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c and ZmLHT14 and represses their expression. 3. Knockout of ZmMYB63 increases sucrose and free amino acid accumulation in kernels.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function editing could enhance kernel carbon and nitrogen accumulation; promising repressor target for breeding.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of UB3 in the SL signalling cascade.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 by binding GTAC motifs in their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functionally part of the D53-UB3 SL signalling module
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone signalling downstream of ZmCCD8; D53 restrains UB3 activity when SL is low
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: kernel carbon and nitrogen accumulation supporting ear growth
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated in ZmCCD8-OE and ZmD14B-OE kernels
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: kernel stage 7 DAP for expression and promoter-binding analyses
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of transporter-repressor module for kernel assimilate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key SL-responsive transcriptional repressor of MYB negative regulators, enabling sugar and amino acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; expression analysis in ZmCCD8 and ZmD14B backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses ZmMYB42/63, thereby indirectly promoting kernel sucrose and amino acid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmUB3 binds GTAC promoter elements of ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63. 2. ZmUB3 represses ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63 transcription in planta. 3. ZmUB3 is positioned downstream of SL signalling and upstream of transporter derepression in kernels.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive regulatory node to enhance kernel assimilate accumulation if activated appropriately within SL signalling context.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct transgenic phenotype of UB3 alone on metabolites was reported in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: SL perception leading to downstream signalling and derepression of UB3 activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Down-regulates ZmMYB42 and ZmMYB63; up-regulates ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c, ZmLHT14 and ZmUB3 expression in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ZmMYB42; ZmMYB63; ZmSWEET10; ZmSWEET13c; ZmLHT14; ZmUB3
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in the SL receptor/signalling module with D53
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Overexpressed in transgenic kernels at 7 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: kernel grain filling stage at 7 and 21 DAP
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SL signal perception controlling kernel sugar and amino acid transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive SL-signalling component sufficient to enhance sucrose and amino acid accumulation in kernels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in maize; metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR of downstream genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sucrose and free amino acid accumulation in kernels
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmD14B overexpression increased soluble sugar and free amino acid concentrations in 21-DAP kernels. 2. It decreased ZmMYB42/63 expression and increased ZmSWEET10, ZmSWEET13c, ZmLHT14 and ZmUB3 expression. 3. This validates that SL perception is sufficient to drive the ZmCCD8 downstream nutrient-accumulation pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering enhanced grain filling and nitrogen efficiency through SL signalling activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study identifies ZmD14B as the relevant SL receptor branch tested in maize kernels.
